SEQUENCE.COMMENT: z_getFirstAvailableQUEDFORDELVTrans - get the first available document that is queued for delivery - be VERY careful modifying any of this logic
this service takes sFwTransactionid as input but potentially returns a sNewTransactionid as output
it uses oracle's FOR UPDATE NOWAIT feature to lock a set of related transactions (documents) that are ready to be delivered (status=QUEUEDFORDELIVERY)
and then also queries the number of these related documents that are actively being delivered (either status=DELIVERING or INLOOP)
Note that this service must use the transactional connection to oracle, since it requires the use of ART transactions which
need a connection setting of LOCAL_TRANSACTION and which cannot be shared with other users of the same connection
returns:
-1 internal error
-2 no documents in QUEUEDFORDELIVERY status
-3 the number of documents concurrently DELIVERING has been met
-4 "ORA-00054: resource busy and acquire with NOWAIT specified or timeout expired" message was returned
-5 Thread is switching to transaction %sNewTransactionid%
-6 Thread continuing with %sFwTransactionid%
Note also that this service will update the SEC_QUEUED value of the transaction, which is why there is no updateActiveMetricQueued service

MAP.COMMENT: sAvailTrans = -1 sSaveTransactionid = sFwTransactionid (the transaction id is restored after the ORALOCK sequence below)
SELECT TO_CHAR(COUNT(*)) AS SCNT FROM TFW_TRANS_ACTIVE WHERE RECEIVER_TP_ALIAS='%critRcv%' AND STATUS='DELIVERING'

Values.value: SELECT TO_CHAR(COUNT(*)) AS SCNT FROM TFW_TRANS_ACTIVE WHERE RECEIVER_TP_ALIAS='%critRcv%' AND STATUS='DELIVERING'

BRANCH.COMMENT: if the number of DELIVERING exceeds CONCURRENCY return -3 otherwise check QUEUEDFORDELIVERY

SEQUENCE.NAME: sCnt >= maxDeliveryThreads
SEQUENCE.COMMENT: -3 if the DELIVERING count is = or > than the max allowed, we definitely need to drop out

Values.value: -3 sAvailTrans has been set to %sAvailTrans%

SEQUENCE.NAME: $default
SEQUENCE.COMMENT: check QUEUEDFORDELIVERY

MAP.COMMENT: SELECT TRANS_ID FROM TFW_TRANS_ACTIVE WHERE RECEIVER_TP_ALIAS='%critRcv%' AND STATUS='QUEUEDFORDELIVERY' FOR UPDATE WAIT 120 ORDER BY TRANSACTION_ID

INVOKE.COMMENT: SELECTED sRows=%sRows% where R='%critRcv%' S='QUEUEDFORDELIVERY' getting back NTID='%sNewTransactionid%' also SCNT=%sCnt% where S='DELIVERING'

Values.value: SELECTED sRows=%sRows% where R='%critRcv%' S='QUEUEDFORDELIVERY' getting back NTID='%sNewTransactionid%' also SCNT=%sCnt% where S='DELIVERING'

SEQUENCE.COMMENT: -2 if the number of QUEUEDFORDELIVERY is 0, then either they have all been delivered, or are being delivered, so we have nothing to do

Values.value: -2 sAvailTrans has been set to %sAvailTrans%

SEQUENCE.NAME: $default
SEQUENCE.COMMENT: 0 we have both a valid number of DELIVERING and a legit new transaction id, so we need to deliver this document

MAP.COMMENT: UPDATE TFW_TRANS_ACTIVE SET STATUS='%newDocStatus%', STATUS_MESSAGE='Document is being delivered', STATUS_DATE=SYSTIMESTAMP,SEQ_QUEUED=SEC_QUEUED+(SYSTIMESTAMP-STATUS_DATE) WHERE TRANSACTION_ID='%sNewTransactionid%'

Values.value: UPDATE TFW_TRANS_ACTIVE SET STATUS='%newDocStatus%', STATUS_MESSAGE='Document is being delivered', STATUS_DATE=to_char(SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP), 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS.FF3TZR'), SEC_QUEUED = to_char(SEC_QUEUED + (with t as (select (SYSTIMESTAMP - TO_TIMESTAMP_TZ('%sQueuedDate%','YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS.FF TZR')) as timediff from DUAL) SELECT extract(day from timediff) * 86400 + extract(hour from timediff) * 3600 + extract(minute from timediff) * 60 + extract(second from timediff) * 1 FROM t), 'FM00000009.900') WHERE TRANSACTION_ID='%sNewTransactionid%'

MAP.COMMENT: INSERT INTO TFW_TRANS_STEPS SELECT '%sNewTransactionid%', TRANSACTION_STEPS_SEQ.NEXTVAL,..., CURRENT_WORKFLOW_STEP,... FROM TFW_TRANS_ACTIVE WHERE TRANSACTION_ID='%sNewTransactionid%'

Values.value: INSERT INTO TFW_TRANS_STEPS SELECT '%sNewTransactionid%', TRANSACTION_STEPS_SEQ.NEXTVAL, '%instanceSpec%', '%sTid%', to_char(SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP), 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS.FF3TZR'), CURRENT_WORKFLOW_STEP, '%newDocStatus%', 'Document is being delivered' FROM TFW_TRANS_ACTIVE WHERE TRANSACTION_ID='%sNewTransactionid%'
0 sAvailTrans has been set to %sAvailTrans%

INVOKE.COMMENT: transaction has been committed with sAvailTrans set to %sAvailTrans%

Values.value: transaction has been committed with sAvailTrans set to %sAvailTrans%
%lastError/pipeline/sFwTransactionid%
sAvailTrans has been set to %sAvailTrans%
sAvailTrans is now %sAvailTrans%

SEQUENCE.COMMENT: sNewTransactionid has been set above

BRANCH.COMMENT: check if the transaction id has changed so that we can return a -5 or a -6

SEQUENCE.NAME: sNewTransactionid != sFwTransactionid
SEQUENCE.COMMENT: return a -5

Values.value: Thread is switching to transaction %sNewTransactionid%
Thread has switched from %sSaveTransactionid%

INVOKE.COMMENT: Transaction status set to %newDocStatus%, message to 'Document is being delivered'

Values.value: Transaction status set to %newDocStatus%, message to 'Document is being delivered'

SEQUENCE.NAME: $default
SEQUENCE.COMMENT: return a -6

Values.value: No thread switch, continuing with %sFwTransactionid%

INVOKE.COMMENT: Transaction status set to %newDocStatus%, message to 'Document is being delivered'

Values.value: Transaction status set to %newDocStatus%, message to 'Document is being delivered'

SEQUENCE.COMMENT: internal error

INVOKE.COMMENT: FE0000 Internal error - LOCK NOWAIT logic failure - further investigation needed

Values.value: FE0000 Internal error - LOCK NOWAIT logic failure - further investigation needed

SEQUENCE.COMMENT: no documents in QUEUEDFORDELIVERY status
the number of documents concurrently DELIVERING has been met
catch block was hit above

INVOKE.COMMENT: sPos = indexOf( sErrmsg, "ORA-00054: resource busy and acquire with NOWAIT specified or timeout expired" )

Values.value: ORA-00054: resource busy and acquire with NOWAIT specified or timeout expired
FE0000 General database failure: %sErrmsg%

MAP.NAME: $default
MAP.COMMENT: we were blocked getting the records from the database, so a retry is required by the caller
